data.title: Worktree/Branch Session Tracking via Ticket Comments
data.description:
## Context
When implementation sub-agents and sprint/fix-bug orchestrators begin work, there is no record of which branch or worktree holds their in-progress changes. Ticket comments track checkpoint progress via CHECKPOINT:phase-name prefixes but not branch location. When a session is interrupted — whether by a crash or manual restart — the resume logic can only see checkpoint level and cannot discover where the work lives. Without branch tracking, resuming a ticket requires manual intervention or blind restarts, discarding recoverable work or duplicating effort already completed. Tracking and recovery must ship together: branch comments without resume logic produce unused data; resume logic without branch comments has nothing to act on.

## Success Criteria
- When an implementation sub-agent begins work on a task ticket, it posts a WORKTREE_TRACKING:start comment recording its branch name and the orchestrator's session branch before any implementation begins.
- When sprint or fix-bug begins a session on an epic or bug ticket, it posts a WORKTREE_TRACKING:start comment recording the session branch before dispatching sub-agents.
- When work completes via harvest-worktree.sh (on any exit path — success or failure), a WORKTREE_TRACKING:complete comment is written with the branch name and outcome (merged or discarded). If harvest exits abnormally before writing the complete comment, the start comment remains unmatched and resume logic applies on next invocation.
- When resuming a ticket with an unmatched WORKTREE_TRACKING:start comment (no corresponding complete), the orchestrator autonomously assesses the abandoned branch: merges it into the current session branch if it contains at least one commit not reachable from the session branch HEAD and the merge produces no conflicts; discards it if already merged (no unique commits) or if any merge conflict requires manual resolution.
- When two competing unmerged implementations exist on a ticket, the orchestrator selects the better one using this priority order: (1) whichever implementation's diff addresses more acceptance criteria items — where acceptance criteria are markdown task-list items (- [ ] or - [x]) in the ticket description and a criterion is considered addressed when its verbatim text appears in the branch's diff; (2) test-gate-status artifact as tiebreaker when criteria counts are equal; (3) fewest merge conflicts as the final tiebreaker; (4) most recently started by WORKTREE_TRACKING:start comment timestamp as the ultimate fallback.
- When a WORKTREE_TRACKING:start references a branch that no longer exists, the orchestrator skips it without error and proceeds.
- An integration test fixture stages an interrupted-session state (a task ticket with an unmatched WORKTREE_TRACKING:start comment pointing to a branch with unmerged commits) and verifies that resuming the ticket results in an autonomous merge or discard decision without any manual git commands.
- After a story's or bug's changes are merged to main, a WORKTREE_TRACKING:landed comment is written on that ticket confirming the merge completed. For tasks, WORKTREE_TRACKING:complete outcome=merged serves as the equivalent landed signal when the task worktree merges into the session worktree.
- When resuming, the orchestrator scans for WORKTREE_TRACKING:start comments on both the top-level epic/bug ticket AND all child story and task tickets. Any ticket — open or closed — that has a start but lacks a corresponding complete (tasks) or landed (stories/bugs) is identified as having potentially unmerged changes and evaluated for recovery.
- When ticket comment writes fail (e.g., .tickets-tracker/ unavailable), WORKTREE_TRACKING writes fail silently without blocking sub-agent work; resume skips tracking-based assessment and falls back to checkpoint-only behavior.
- When resuming a ticket with no WORKTREE_TRACKING:start comment, the orchestrator proceeds with existing checkpoint-only resume behavior unchanged.

## Dependencies
None

## Approach
Add WORKTREE_TRACKING:start (unique per branch+timestamp, stable identity anchor) and WORKTREE_TRACKING:complete comment writes at the start of sub-agent/orchestrator work and at harvest (on any exit path) respectively, using the existing ticket comment CLI. Add WORKTREE_TRACKING:landed writes to the story/bug merge-to-main flow. Resume logic in sprint and fix-bug scans for unmatched start comments across both the top-level ticket and child story and task tickets, checks git state for each identified branch, and auto-decides recovery path using the acceptance-criteria (verbatim task-list match) -> test-status -> conflict-count -> timestamp tiebreaker cascade. Multiple unmatched starts from different branches trigger the competing-implementation tiebreak; multiple starts from the same branch are de-duplicated by branch name. Comment writes degrade gracefully when .tickets-tracker/ is unavailable.

## Scenario Analysis
- Race condition: two sub-agents write WORKTREE_TRACKING:start simultaneously (high, runtime): Two parallel sub-agents on the same ticket both write start comments; resume triggers competing-implementation tiebreak via SC5 cascade.
- WORKTREE_TRACKING:complete never written when harvest exits non-zero (high, runtime): Addressed by SC3 covering all exit paths; unmatched start = abandoned branch = recovery evaluation.
- Branch name stored in comment becomes stale after cleanup (high, runtime): Addressed by SC6 (graceful skip) and SC9 (start/no-landed signal flags inconsistency).
- Competing tiebreak non-deterministic when all tied (medium, runtime): Addressed by SC5 timestamp fallback as ultimate tiebreaker.
- Acceptance criteria evaluation fails under context pressure (medium, runtime): Addressed by SC5 specifying verbatim static checklist comparison — no LLM judgment required for criteria counting.
- Existing in-flight tickets have no tracking comment after deploy (high, deployment): Addressed by SC11 — no tracking comment = checkpoint-only fallback.
- Ticket comment schema has no version field (high, deployment): Implementation plan must specify a stable parseable key=value format.
- harvest-worktree.sh has no hook point for complete comment (high, deployment): Implementation plan decision: write from orchestrator after harvest call vs. from within harvest script.
- No config flag to disable WORKTREE_TRACKING (high, configuration): Addressed by SC10 — writes fail silently, no blocking.
- Branch name special characters break parsing (medium, configuration): Implementation plan must specify an escaping strategy.
- Resume scan has no staleness threshold (medium, configuration): Handled by git state check (already merged = no unique commits = discard path) plus SC6/SC9.
- WORKTREE_TRACKING:start on task but resume scans epic only (high, configuration): Addressed by SC9 requiring dual-level scanning.
- Closed ticket with unmerged changes indistinguishable from clean close (high, configuration): Addressed by SC8+SC9 — landed comment + resume scan across open/closed tickets.

## Prompt Alignment Findings
- Matched signal: skill file modifications (sprint/fix-bug SKILL.md)
- LangGraph sentinel pattern: use stable unique identity anchor (branch+timestamp) for matching, not fuzzy content scan. Incorporated in Approach.
- OpenAI Swarm explicit conditionals: merge/discard decision encoded as ranked ruleset in SKILL.md, not open-ended LLM judgment. SC4 and SC5 implement deterministic cascades.
- Multi-agent research: concurrent start-comment races use timestamp fallback for deterministic resolution rather than open-ended assessment.
